title: Can Playing Video Games Help You Get a Better Job
subtitle: 玩游戏能帮你找到更好的工作吗

paragraphs.en: The gaming industry is larger than films and music combined, yet few of us are likely to list our achievements in playing computer games as work experience in our résumé. But why not? Businesses are waking up to the skills gamers can bring to the workplace.
paragraphs.zh: 游戏行业的规模超过电影与音乐的总和，然而很少有人会把玩电脑游戏的成就写进简历作为工作经验。但为什么不呢？企业正逐渐意识到游戏玩家能为职场带来的种种技能。

paragraphs.en: One start-up is convinced that the skills learned by playing games — hard-won through years of training and battle — can be applied to real-life work situations. And Game Academy reckons its belief is backed up by hard data. Do you enjoy unusual puzzle games like Portal, or tower defence games like Defense Grid? The team has found that IT workers play those more than average.
paragraphs.zh: 一家初创企业坚信，通过玩游戏磨练出的技能——历经多年训练与对战艰难习得——完全可以应用于现实工作场景。游戏学院认为这一信念有确凿数据支撑。你喜欢Portal这类独特的解谜游戏，还是Defense Grid这样的塔防游戏？该团队发现，IT从业者玩这类游戏的频率高于平均水平。

words.meaning: adj. 来之不易的；艰苦获得的

paragraphs.en: But if you prefer Civilization, Total War, or X-Com, where strategy and resource management are key, then you might have more in common with managers. Game Academy's idea is simple: analyse gamers' habits from their online gaming profile, and offer courses in valuable skills that reflect their aptitudes — skills they can practise and refine in-game. And there is already a growing acceptance that gaming skills are transferable.
paragraphs.zh: 而如果你更偏爱《文明》、《全面战争》或《幽浮》这类以战略和资源管理为核心的游戏，那你可能与管理者有更多共同之处。游戏学院的思路很简单：通过分析玩家的在线游戏档案来了解其习惯，并据此提供与其才能相符的技能课程——这些技能可以在游戏中反复练习和精进。而游戏技能可以迁移应用的观点，正获得越来越广泛的认可。

words.meaning: n. 天资；才能；适应能力
adj. 可转移的；可迁移的

paragraphs.en: Even the military is hiring gamers. "The ability to absorb information, react swiftly and coordinate actions whilst remaining calm under pressure are often attributes of people that are good at gaming," according to a Royal Air Force spokesperson. Those skills are part of what the Royal Air Force is looking for "in a variety of roles". "Skills acquired through gaming can be very relevant to certain areas."
paragraphs.zh: 就连军队也开始招募游戏玩家。英国皇家空军发言人表示："善于游戏的人往往具备快速吸收信息、迅速反应和协调行动的能力，同时能在压力下保持冷静。"这些技能正是皇家空军在"多种岗位"中所寻求的。"通过游戏获得的技能，与某些领域的工作高度相关。"

paragraphs.en: "There are plenty of soft skills that gamers can utilise in a professional setting, such as teamwork, problem solving and strategic planning," says Ryan Gardner, a regional director with Hays recruitment. But does that really mean your top 100 ranking in Overwatch should go down in your résumé? "It's about how you either make it relevant to the job you are applying for, or how it makes you more interesting as a potential employee," Mr Gardner says.
paragraphs.zh: 海斯招聘区域总监Ryan Gardner表示："游戏玩家可以在职业环境中运用许多软技能，例如团队合作、问题解决和战略规划。"但这是否真的意味着你在《守望先锋》中跻身前100名的排名应该写进简历？Gardner先生说："关键在于你如何将其与所申请的职位挂钩，或者它如何让你作为潜在员工显得更具吸引力。"

paragraphs.en: Two years ago, a Glasgow University study made headlines for suggesting gaming could make students more successful. But "the research hasn't really changed minds, at least not yet," says one of the authors of the study, Dr Matthew Barr. He's now writing a book on the topic. "For now, I think the negative reputation around games means that telling someone you're a determined gamer is more likely to harm your chances of getting a job. But if someone can explain how they've led a team in an online game, for example, they may be able to persuade an employer that this is useful," says Dr Barr.
paragraphs.zh: 两年前，格拉斯哥大学一项研究因提出游戏能让学生更易成功而登上头条。但该研究作者之一Matthew Barr博士表示："这项研究并没有真正改变人们的看法，至少目前还没有。"他目前正就这一课题撰写一本书。"就目前而言，游戏的负面声誉意味着，告诉别人你是一名铁杆游戏玩家，更可能损害你的求职机会。但如果有人能解释他们如何在网络游戏中带领团队，那他们或许能说服雇主这项经历是有价值的，"Barr博士说。

paragraphs.en: Playing games casually is unlikely to help your career prospects. But part of Game Academy's suggestion is to transform casual gamers into "conscious" ones, applying critical thinking to developing their skills. "We see gameplay as a resource of talent," says co-founder David Barrie. "Why can't we put in our résumé our gaming victories and achievements?" he asks. "If they say they want leadership — why can't I point to my years of leading World of Warcraft raids?"
paragraphs.zh: 随意玩游戏不太可能对你的职业前景有所帮助。但游戏学院的建议之一，正是将休闲玩家转变为"有意识"的玩家，将批判性思维运用于技能的发展之中。"我们将游戏视为一种人才资源，"联合创始人David Barrie说道。"为什么我们不能把游戏中的胜利与成就写进简历呢？"他问道。"如果他们说希望看到领导力——为什么我不能以多年带领《魔兽世界》团队副本的经历来佐证？"

words.meaning: n. 批判性思维；理性分析能力

paragraphs.en: There are already plenty of gamers out there who know that their hobby has made them who they are. Earlier this year, Matthew Ricci told gaming site Kotaku: "If you're playing EVE Online you basically already have an MBA." Often made fun of in gaming circles as a "spreadsheet simulator", the economy of the fictional EVE universe is driven by real market principles. If you want to build a new spaceship, the raw material has to be mined by another player. Manufacturing costs come into effect, and commodities fluctuate in price based on demand and the distance to haul the commodity.
paragraphs.zh: 已经有不少游戏玩家深知，正是这一爱好塑造了今天的自己。今年早些时候，Matthew Ricci告诉游戏网站Kotaku："如果你在玩《EVE Online》，你基本上已经相当于拿到了MBA学位。"这款游戏在玩家圈中常被戏称为"电子表格模拟器"，而虚构的EVE宇宙经济体系却由真实的市场原则驱动。想要建造一艘新飞船，原材料就必须由其他玩家开采，制造成本随之产生，商品价格也会依据需求和运输距离而起伏波动。

paragraphs.en: Mr Ricci, who had always dreamed of being the boss of his own company, ran an in-game corporation comprising hundreds of players. Eventually, he realised he could transfer his skills to real-life business — instead of doing it for free. He restructured Zentech, once a taxation vehicle for his father's business, and it is now in its fourth year helping international brands enter the Canadian market. EVE teaches skills like creativity, leadership, organisation, and conflict resolution, he maintains. He credits his success to his family, his strong desire to run his own business — and "a damn good company in Iceland that made a damn good game".
paragraphs.zh: 里奇先生一直梦想拥有自己的公司，他曾在游戏中管理一家由数百名玩家组成的公司。最终他意识到，自己完全可以把这些技能迁移到现实商业中，而不只是免费练手。他重组了Zentech——这家公司曾是其父亲企业的税务工具——如今已步入第四个年头，专注于帮助国际品牌进入加拿大市场。他坚持认为，《EVE》传授了创造力、领导力、组织能力和冲突解决等技能。他将自己的成功归功于家人、强烈的创业意愿，以及"冰岛那家做出了一款绝佳游戏的了不起的公司"。

words.meaning: n. 冲突解决；矛盾调处

paragraphs.en: Digital entrepreneur and business consultant Mia Bennett says: "In more traditional settings, gaming is still imagined to be the pursuit of teenage boys — a waste of time." But there are some links to skills like "decision-making, the ability to anticipate and future planning," she says. "It also helps with meta-skills — learning how to learn, experimentation, and creative thinking."
paragraphs.zh: 数字企业家兼商业顾问Mia Bennett表示："在较为传统的环境中，游戏仍被视为青少年男孩的消遣——浪费时间。"但她指出，游戏与"决策能力、预判能力和未来规划"等技能之间存在一定关联。"它还有助于培养元技能——学会如何学习、勇于尝试以及创造性思维。"

words.meaning: v. 预判；预期；提前考虑

paragraphs.en: Twelve years into his career, Mark Long, a radiotherapy physicist with the NHS in Surrey, doesn't get as much gaming time as he used to. "I would love to say that my gaming skills allow me to destroy cancer using linear accelerators like photon blasters, but that's not quite the reality." Instead, he credits old-school games like Palace of Magic with exposing him to computers. Every new gaming upgrade improved his knowledge of how they worked.
paragraphs.zh: 从业十二年的Mark Long是萨里郡NHS的一名放射治疗物理学家，他已没有过去那么多时间玩游戏了。"我很想说，我的游戏技能让我能像操控光子炮一样用直线加速器消灭癌细胞，但现实并非如此。"他将自己与计算机结缘归功于《魔宫》等老派游戏，每一次游戏升级都加深了他对计算机运作原理的理解。

paragraphs.en: Gaming also encouraged a competitive spirit — and that, he believes, translates. "When creating treatment plans, the aim is to optimise the radiation dose to the tumour and restrict it as much as possible to healthy surrounding tissues and organs," he says. It's about "repeating the process, but each time doing something slightly different to improve the result". Not unlike achieving a high score or a perfect run. "Most videogames are essentially puzzles to solve," he says. "And problem-solving is a big part of my job."
paragraphs.zh: 游戏还培养了竞争意识——而他认为这一点同样可以迁移到现实中。"制定治疗方案时，目标是优化对肿瘤的辐射剂量，同时尽可能将辐射限制在周围的健康组织和器官之外，"他说。这就是"不断重复这一过程，但每次都做出细微调整以改善结果"。这与追求高分或完美通关并无二致。"大多数电子游戏本质上都是需要解决的谜题，"他说，"而解决问题正是我工作的重要组成部分。"

paragraphs.en: Over at Game Academy, Mr Barrie is aware they still have a mountain to climb. "The employer community will need the science," he says, but confesses he only became a true believer when he started asking players about their biggest in-game achievements. "The scale and scope of their answers were amazing," he says. "But ask them the same about their work, and they don't know." "The engagement and powerful skills that people get playing a game — why can't work be like that?" he asks.
paragraphs.zh: 在游戏学院，Barrie先生清楚地意识到他们面前仍有一座大山需要攀登。"雇主群体需要看到科学依据，"他说，但他坦言，直到开始询问玩家在游戏中最大的成就时，自己才真正成了坚定的信徒。"他们回答的规模和广度令人叹为观止，"他说，"但问他们同样的问题——工作上的成就——他们却茫然不知。""人们在玩游戏时所获得的投入感和强大技能——为什么工作就不能是这样呢？"他问道。

words.meaning: n. 投入感；参与度；专注
